mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as market participants anticipate lower borrowing costs.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 5% over 3 months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days as investors price in recessionary risks.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high UNRATE environments exceed 1.5% or fail to show significant negative correlation (Pearson r > -0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in GDP and housing starts indicate weakening demand conditions, creating a negative feedback loop that drives S&P 500 returns lower over 60 trading days through reduced corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-declining GDPC1/HOUST scenarios are positive or show no significant negative correlation (Pearson r > -0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals weakening supply chain efficiency, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors discount lower corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO decline periods exceed 1% or show no significant negative correlation (Pearson r > -0.25).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% over 6 months triggers central bank tightening expectations, creating headwinds for equity markets and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high CPI environments exceed 2% or show no significant negative correlation (Pearson r > -0.2).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 150k jobs per month signals a tightening labor market, pushing wages higher and creating inflationary pressures that lead to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high PAYEMS environments exceed 1.5% or show no significant negative correlation (Pearson r > -0.2).

mechanism_narrative: A surge in real estate financing activity above its 3-month average indicates strong consumer and business confidence, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved economic outlooks.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high RSAFS environments fall below 0.5% or show no significant positive correlation (Pearson r < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising inflation and stagnant interest rates creates real rate compression, driving S&P 500 returns higher over 20 trading days as investors seek nominal growth assets.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in CPI-FEDFUNDS divergence scenarios are negative or show no significant positive correlation (Pearson r < 0.15).
